tables.name: account_policy_data
tables.description: Additional macOS user account data from the AccountPolicy section of OpenDirectory.

tables.name: acpi_tables
tables.description: Firmware ACPI functional table common metadata and content.

tables.name: ad_config
tables.description: macOS Active Directory configuration.

tables.name: alf
tables.description: macOS application layer firewall (ALF) service details.

tables.name: alf_exceptions
tables.description: macOS application layer firewall (ALF) service exceptions.

tables.name: alf_explicit_auths
tables.description: ALF services explicitly allowed to perform networking. Not supported on macOS 15+ (returns no results).

tables.name: app_schemes
tables.description: macOS application schemes and handlers (e.g., http, file, mailto).

tables.name: apparmor_events
tables.description: Track AppArmor events.

tables.name: apparmor_profiles
tables.description: Track active AppArmor profiles.

tables.name: appcompat_shims
tables.description: Application Compatibility shims are a way to persist malware. This table presents the AppCompat Shim information from the registry in a nice format. See http://files.brucon.org/2015/Tomczak_and_Ballenthin_Shims_for_the_Win.pdf for more details.

tables.name: apps
tables.description: macOS applications installed in known search paths (e.g., /Applications).

tables.name: apt_sources
tables.description: Current list of APT repositories or software channels.

tables.name: arp_cache
tables.description: Address resolution cache, both static and dynamic (from ARP, NDP).

tables.name: asl
tables.description: Queries the Apple System Log data structure for system events.

tables.name: augeas
tables.description: Configuration files parsed by augeas.

tables.name: authenticode
tables.description: File (executable, bundle, installer, disk) code signing status.

tables.name: authorization_mechanisms
tables.description: macOS Authorization mechanisms database.

tables.name: authorizations
tables.description: macOS Authorization rights database.

tables.name: authorized_keys
tables.description: A line-delimited authorized_keys table.

tables.name: autoexec
tables.description: Aggregate of executables that will automatically execute on the target machine. This is an amalgamation of other tables like services, scheduled_tasks, startup_items and more.

tables.name: azure_instance_metadata
tables.description: Azure instance metadata.

tables.name: azure_instance_tags
tables.description: Azure instance tags.

tables.name: background_activities_moderator
tables.description: Background Activities Moderator (BAM) tracks application execution.

tables.name: battery
tables.description: Provides information about the internal battery of a laptop. Note: On Windows, columns with Ah or mAh units assume that the battery is 12V.

tables.name: bitlocker_info
tables.description: Retrieve bitlocker status of the machine.

tables.name: block_devices
tables.description: Block (buffered access) device file nodes: disks, ramdisks, and DMG containers.

tables.name: bpf_process_events
tables.description: Track time/action process executions.

tables.name: bpf_socket_events
tables.description: Track network socket opens and closes.

tables.name: browser_plugins
tables.description: All C/NPAPI browser plugin details for all users. C/NPAPI has been deprecated on all major browsers. To query for plugins on modern browsers, try: `chrome_extensions` `firefox_addons` `safari_extensions`.

tables.name: carbon_black_info
tables.description: Returns info about a Carbon Black sensor install.

tables.name: carves
tables.description: List the set of completed and in-progress carves. If carve=1 then the query is treated as a new carve request.

tables.name: certificate_trust_settings
tables.description: Certificate Authorities trust settings installed in Keychains/ca-bundles.

tables.name: certificates
tables.description: Certificate Authorities installed in Keychains/ca-bundles. NOTE: osquery limits frequent access to keychain files on macOS. This limit is controlled by keychain_access_interval flag. On macOS, 'path' may point to either a keychain file or a DER/PEM-encoded certificate file; non-keychain files are parsed as DER/PEM.

tables.name: chassis_info
tables.description: Display information pertaining to the chassis and its security status.

tables.name: chocolatey_packages
tables.description: Chocolatey packages installed in a system.

tables.name: chrome_extension_content_scripts
tables.description: Chrome browser extension content scripts.

tables.name: chrome_extensions
tables.description: Chrome-based browser extensions.

tables.name: connected_displays
tables.description: Provides information about the connected displays of the machine.

tables.name: connectivity
tables.description: Provides the overall system's network state.

tables.name: cpu_info
tables.description: Retrieve cpu hardware info of the machine.

tables.name: cpu_time
tables.description: Displays information from /proc/stat file about the time the cpu cores spent in different parts of the system.

tables.name: cpuid
tables.description: Useful CPU features from the cpuid ASM call.

tables.name: crashes
tables.description: Application, System, and Mobile App crash logs.

tables.name: crontab
tables.description: Line parsed values from system and user cron/tab.

tables.name: cups_destinations
tables.description: Returns all configured printers.

tables.name: cups_jobs
tables.description: Returns all completed print jobs from cups.

tables.name: curl
tables.description: Perform an http request and return stats about it.

tables.name: curl_certificate
tables.description: Inspect TLS certificates by connecting to input hostnames.

tables.name: deb_package_files
tables.description: Installed files from DEB packages that are currently installed on the system.

tables.name: deb_packages
tables.description: The installed DEB package database.

tables.name: default_environment
tables.description: Default environment variables and values.

tables.name: device_file
tables.description: Similar to the file table, but use TSK and allow block address access.

tables.name: device_firmware
tables.description: A best-effort list of discovered firmware versions.

tables.name: device_hash
tables.description: Similar to the hash table, but use TSK and allow block address access.

tables.name: device_partitions
tables.description: Use TSK to enumerate details about partitions on a disk device.

tables.name: deviceguard_status
tables.description: Retrieve DeviceGuard info of the machine.

tables.name: disk_encryption
tables.description: Disk encryption status and information.

tables.name: disk_events
tables.description: Track DMG disk image events (appearance/disappearance) when opened.

tables.name: disk_info
tables.description: Retrieve basic information about the physical disks of a system.

tables.name: dns_cache
tables.description: Enumerate the DNS cache using the undocumented DnsGetCacheDataTable function in dnsapi.dll.

tables.name: dns_lookup_events
tables.description: DNS lookups performed through the Windows DNS stack.

tables.name: dns_resolvers
tables.description: Resolvers used by this host. Note: On Windows this data is available in the interface_details table.

tables.name: docker_container_envs
tables.description: Docker container environment variables.

tables.name: docker_container_fs_changes
tables.description: Changes to files or directories on container's filesystem.

tables.name: docker_container_labels
tables.description: Docker container labels.

tables.name: docker_container_mounts
tables.description: Docker container mounts.

tables.name: docker_container_networks
tables.description: Docker container networks.

tables.name: docker_container_ports
tables.description: Docker container ports.

tables.name: docker_container_processes
tables.description: Docker container processes.

tables.name: docker_container_stats
tables.description: Docker container statistics. Queries on this table take at least one second.

tables.name: docker_containers
tables.description: Docker containers information.

tables.name: docker_image_history
tables.description: Docker image history information.

tables.name: docker_image_labels
tables.description: Docker image labels.

tables.name: docker_image_layers
tables.description: Docker image layers information.

tables.name: docker_images
tables.description: Docker images information.

tables.name: docker_info
tables.description: Docker system information.

tables.description: Docker network labels.

tables.name: docker_networks
tables.description: Docker networks information.

tables.name: docker_version
tables.description: Docker version information.

tables.name: docker_volume_labels
tables.description: Docker volume labels.

tables.name: docker_volumes
tables.description: Docker volumes information.

tables.name: drivers
tables.description: Details for in-use Windows device drivers. This does not display installed but unused drivers.

tables.name: ec2_instance_metadata
tables.description: EC2 instance metadata.

tables.name: ec2_instance_tags
tables.description: EC2 instance tag key value pairs.

tables.name: es_process_events
tables.description: Process execution events from EndpointSecurity.

tables.name: es_process_file_events
tables.description: File integrity monitoring events from EndpointSecurity including process context.

tables.name: etc_hosts
tables.description: Line-parsed /etc/hosts.

tables.name: etc_protocols
tables.description: Line-parsed /etc/protocols.

tables.name: etc_services
tables.description: Line-parsed /etc/services.

tables.name: event_taps
tables.description: Returns information about installed event taps.

tables.name: extended_attributes
tables.description: Returns the extended attributes for files (similar to Windows ADS).

tables.name: fan_speed_sensors
tables.description: Fan speeds.

tables.name: file
tables.description: Interactive filesystem attributes and metadata.

tables.name: file_events
tables.description: Track time/action changes to files specified in configuration data.

tables.name: firefox_addons
tables.description: Firefox browser extensions, webapps, and addons.

tables.name: gatekeeper

tables.name: gatekeeper_approved_apps
tables.description: Gatekeeper apps a user has allowed to run.

tables.name: groups
tables.description: Local system groups.

tables.name: hardware_events
tables.description: Hardware (PCI/USB/HID) events from UDEV or IOKit.

tables.name: hash
tables.description: Filesystem hash data.

tables.name: homebrew_packages
tables.description: The installed homebrew package database.

tables.name: ibridge_info
tables.description: Information about the Apple iBridge hardware controller.

tables.name: ie_extensions
tables.description: Internet Explorer browser extensions.

tables.name: intel_me_info
tables.description: Intel ME/CSE Info.

tables.name: interface_addresses
tables.description: Network interfaces and relevant metadata.

tables.name: interface_details
tables.description: Detailed information and stats of network interfaces.

tables.name: interface_ipv6
tables.description: IPv6 configuration and stats of network interfaces.

tables.name: iokit_devicetree
tables.description: The IOKit registry matching the DeviceTree plane.

tables.name: iokit_registry
tables.description: The full IOKit registry without selecting a plane.

tables.name: iptables
tables.description: Linux IP packet filtering and NAT tool.

tables.name: jetbrains_plugins
tables.description: JetBrains IDEs plugins.

tables.name: kernel_extensions
tables.description: macOS's kernel extensions, both loaded and within the load search path.

tables.name: kernel_info
tables.description: Basic active kernel information.

tables.name: kernel_keys
tables.description: List of security data, authentication keys and encryption keys.

tables.name: kernel_modules
tables.description: Linux kernel modules both loaded and within the load search path.

tables.name: kernel_panics
tables.description: System kernel panic logs.

tables.name: keychain_acls
tables.description: Applications that have ACL entries in the keychain. NOTE: osquery limits frequent access to keychain files. This limit is controlled by keychain_access_interval flag.

tables.name: keychain_items
tables.description: Generic details about keychain items. NOTE: osquery limits frequent access to keychain files. This limit is controlled by keychain_access_interval flag.

tables.name: known_hosts
tables.description: A line-delimited known_hosts table.

tables.name: kva_speculative_info
tables.description: Display kernel virtual address and speculative execution information for the system.

tables.name: last
tables.description: System logins and logouts.

tables.name: launchd
tables.description: LaunchAgents and LaunchDaemons from default search paths.

tables.name: location_services
tables.description: Reports the status of the Location Services feature of the OS.

tables.name: logged_in_users
tables.description: Users with an active shell on the system.

tables.name: logical_drives
tables.description: Details for logical drives on the system. A logical drive generally represents a single partition.

tables.name: logon_sessions
tables.description: Windows Logon Session.

tables.name: lxd_storage_pools
tables.description: LXD storage pool information.

tables.name: magic
tables.description: Magic number recognition library table.

tables.name: managed_policies
tables.description: The managed configuration policies from AD, MDM, MCX, etc.

tables.name: md_devices
tables.description: Software RAID array settings.

tables.name: md_drives
tables.description: Drive devices used for Software RAID.

tables.name: md_personalities
tables.description: Software RAID setting supported by the kernel.

tables.name: mdfind
tables.description: Run searches against the spotlight database.

tables.description: Query file metadata in the Spotlight database.

tables.name: memory_array_mapped_addresses
tables.description: Data associated for address mapping of physical memory arrays.

tables.name: memory_arrays
tables.description: Data associated with collection of memory devices that operate to form a memory address.

tables.name: memory_device_mapped_addresses
tables.description: Data associated for address mapping of physical memory devices.

tables.name: memory_devices
tables.description: Physical memory device (type 17) information retrieved from SMBIOS.

tables.name: memory_error_info
tables.description: Data associated with errors of a physical memory array.

tables.name: memory_info
tables.description: Main memory information in bytes.

tables.name: memory_map
tables.description: OS memory region map.

tables.name: mounts
tables.description: System mounted devices and filesystems (not process specific).

tables.name: msr
tables.description: Various pieces of data stored in the model specific register per processor. NOTE: the msr kernel module must be enabled, and osquery must be run as root.

tables.name: nfs_shares
tables.description: NFS shares exported by the host.

tables.name: npm_packages
tables.description: Node packages installed in a system.

tables.name: ntdomains
tables.description: Display basic NT domain information of a Windows machine.

tables.name: ntfs_acl_permissions
tables.description: Retrieve NTFS ACL permission information for files and directories.

tables.name: ntfs_journal_events
tables.description: Track time/action changes to files specified in configuration data.

tables.name: nvram
tables.description: Apple NVRAM variable listing.

tables.name: oem_strings
tables.description: OEM defined strings retrieved from SMBIOS.

tables.name: office_mru
tables.description: View recently opened Office documents.

tables.name: os_version
tables.description: A single row containing the operating system name and version.

tables.name: osquery_events
tables.description: Information about the event publishers and subscribers.

tables.name: osquery_extensions
tables.description: List of active osquery extensions.

tables.name: osquery_flags
tables.description: Configurable flags that modify osquery's behavior.

tables.name: osquery_info
tables.description: Top level information about the running version of osquery.

tables.name: osquery_packs
tables.description: Information about the current query packs that are loaded in osquery.

tables.name: osquery_registry
tables.description: List the osquery registry plugins.

tables.name: osquery_schedule
tables.description: Information about the current queries that are scheduled in osquery.

tables.name: package_bom
tables.description: macOS package bill of materials (BOM) file list.

tables.name: package_install_history
tables.description: macOS package install history.

tables.name: package_receipts
tables.description: macOS package receipt details.

tables.name: password_policy
tables.description: OpenDirectory account policies for macOS including password content, authentication, and password change policies.

tables.name: patches
tables.description: Lists all the patches applied. Note: This does not include patches applied via MSI or downloaded from Windows Update (e.g. Service Packs).

tables.name: pci_devices
tables.description: PCI devices active on the host system.

tables.name: physical_disk_performance
tables.description: Provides provides raw data from performance counters that monitor hard or fixed disk drives on the system.

tables.name: pipes
tables.description: Named and Anonymous pipes.

tables.name: platform_info
tables.description: Information about EFI/UEFI/ROM and platform/boot.

tables.name: plist
tables.description: Read and parse a plist file.

tables.name: portage_keywords
tables.description: A summary about portage configurations like keywords, mask and unmask.

tables.name: portage_packages
tables.description: List of currently installed packages.

tables.name: portage_use
tables.description: List of enabled portage USE values for specific package.

tables.name: power_sensors
tables.description: Machine power (currents, voltages, wattages, etc) sensors.

tables.name: powershell_events
tables.description: Powershell script blocks reconstructed to their full script content, this table requires script block logging to be enabled.

tables.name: preferences
tables.description: macOS defaults and managed preferences.

tables.name: prefetch
tables.description: Prefetch files show metadata related to file execution.

tables.name: process_envs
tables.description: A key/value table of environment variables for each process.

tables.name: process_etw_events
tables.description: Windows process execution events.

tables.name: process_events
tables.description: Track time/action process executions.

tables.name: process_file_events
tables.description: A File Integrity Monitor implementation using the audit service.

tables.name: process_memory_map
tables.description: Process memory mapped files and pseudo device/regions.

tables.name: process_namespaces
tables.description: Linux namespaces for processes running on the host system.

tables.name: process_open_files
tables.description: File descriptors for each process.

tables.name: process_open_handles NEW
tables.description: Enumerate open handles for a specified process. Defaults to the osquery process if no pid constraint is provided.

tables.name: process_open_pipes
tables.description: Pipes and partner processes for each process.

tables.name: process_open_sockets
tables.description: Processes which have open network sockets on the system.

tables.name: processes
tables.description: All running processes on the host system.

tables.name: programs
tables.description: Represents products as they are installed by Windows Installer. A product generally correlates to one installation package on Windows. Some fields may be blank as Windows installation details are left to the discretion of the product author.

tables.name: prometheus_metrics
tables.description: Retrieve metrics from a Prometheus server.

tables.name: python_packages
tables.description: Python packages installed in a system. NOTE: when querying on windows, even without a users cross join, all user installed python packages will be returned. This special behavior is to not break original functionality.

tables.name: quicklook_cache
tables.description: Files and thumbnails within macOS's Quicklook Cache.

tables.name: recent_files
tables.description: Recently files (as displayed in Start Menu or File Explorer).

tables.name: registry
tables.description: All of the Windows registry hives.

tables.name: routes
tables.description: The active route table for the host system.

tables.name: rpm_package_files
tables.description: Installed files from RPM packages that are currently installed on the system.

tables.name: rpm_packages
tables.description: RPM packages that are currently installed on the host system.

tables.name: running_apps
tables.description: macOS applications currently running on the host system.

tables.name: safari_extensions
tables.description: Safari browser extension details for all users. This table requires Full Disk Access (FDA) permission.

tables.name: sandboxes
tables.description: macOS application sandboxes container details.

tables.name: scheduled_tasks
tables.description: Lists all of the tasks in the Windows task scheduler.

tables.name: screenlock
tables.description: macOS screenlock status. Note: only fetches results for osquery's current logged-in user context. The user must also have recently logged in.

tables.name: seccomp_events
tables.description: A virtual table that tracks seccomp events.

tables.name: secureboot
tables.description: Secure Boot UEFI Settings.

tables.name: secureboot_certificates NEW
tables.description: X.509 certificates from UEFI Secure Boot signature databases (db and dbx EFI variables). Useful for monitoring CA expiry and adoption of updated certificates (e.g. Microsoft UEFI CA 2023).

tables.name: security_profile_info
tables.description: Information on the security profile of a given system by listing the system Account and Audit Policies. This table mimics the exported securitypolicy output from the secedit tool.

tables.name: selinux_events
tables.description: Track SELinux events.

tables.name: selinux_settings
tables.description: Track active SELinux settings.

tables.name: services
tables.description: Lists all installed Windows services and their relevant data.

tables.name: shadow
tables.description: Local system users encrypted passwords and related information. Please note, that you usually need superuser rights to access `/etc/shadow`.

tables.name: shared_folders
tables.description: Folders available to others via SMB or AFP.

tables.name: shared_memory
tables.description: OS shared memory regions.

tables.name: shared_resources
tables.description: Displays shared resources on a computer system running Windows. This may be a disk drive, printer, interprocess communication, or other sharable device.

tables.name: sharing_preferences
tables.description: macOS Sharing preferences.

tables.name: shell_history
tables.description: A line-delimited (command) table of per-user .*_history data.

tables.name: shellbags
tables.description: Shows directories accessed via Windows Explorer.

tables.name: shimcache
tables.description: Application Compatibility Cache, contains artifacts of execution.

tables.name: signature
tables.description: File (executable, bundle, installer, disk) code signing status.

tables.name: sip_config
tables.description: Apple's System Integrity Protection (rootless) status.

tables.name: smbios_tables
tables.description: BIOS (DMI) structure common details and content.

tables.name: smc_keys
tables.description: Apple's system management controller keys.

tables.name: socket_events
tables.description: Track network socket bind, connect, and accepts.

tables.name: ssh_configs
tables.description: A table of parsed ssh_configs.

tables.name: startup_items
tables.description: Applications and binaries set as startup items.

tables.name: sudoers
tables.description: Rules for running commands as other users via sudo.

tables.name: suid_bin
tables.description: suid binaries in common locations.

tables.name: system_controls
tables.description: sysctl names, values, and settings information.

tables.name: system_extensions
tables.description: macOS (>= 10.15) system extension table.

tables.name: system_info
tables.description: System information for identification.

tables.name: system_profiler
tables.description: Query system_profiler data types and return the full result as JSON. Returns only the data types specified in the constraints. See available data types with `system_profiler -listDataTypes`.

tables.name: systemd_units
tables.description: Track systemd units.

tables.name: temperature_sensors
tables.description: Machine's temperature sensors.

tables.name: time
tables.description: Track current date and time in UTC.

tables.name: time_machine_backups
tables.description: Backups to drives using TimeMachine. This table requires Full Disk Access (FDA) permission.

tables.name: time_machine_destinations
tables.description: Locations backed up to using Time Machine. This table requires Full Disk Access (FDA) permission.

tables.name: tpm_info
tables.description: A table that lists the TPM related information.

tables.name: ulimit_info
tables.description: System resource usage limits.

tables.name: unified_log
tables.description: Queries the OSLog framework for entries in the system log. The maximum number of rows returned is limited for performance issues. Use timestamp > or >= constraints to optimize query performance. This table introduces a new idiom for extracting sequential data in batches using multiple queries, ordered by timestamp. To trigger it, the user should include the condition "timestamp > -1", and the table will handle pagination. Note that the saved pagination counter is incremented globally across all queries and table invocations within a query. To avoid multiple table invocations within a query, use only AND and = constraints in WHERE clause.

tables.name: uptime
tables.description: Track time passed since last boot. Some systems track this as calendar time, some as runtime.

tables.name: usb_devices
tables.description: USB devices that are actively plugged into the host system.

tables.name: user_events
tables.description: Track user events from the audit framework.

tables.name: user_groups
tables.description: Local system user group relationships.

tables.name: user_interaction_events
tables.description: Track user interaction events from macOS' event tapping framework.

tables.name: user_ssh_keys
tables.description: Returns the private keys in the users ~/.ssh directory and whether or not they are encrypted.

tables.name: userassist
tables.description: UserAssist Registry Key tracks when a user executes an application from Windows Explorer.

tables.name: users
tables.description: Local user accounts (including domain accounts that have logged on locally (Windows)).

tables.name: video_info
tables.description: Retrieve video card information of the machine.

tables.name: virtual_memory_info
tables.description: Darwin Virtual Memory statistics.

tables.name: vscode_extensions
tables.description: Lists all vscode extensions.

tables.name: wifi_networks
tables.description: macOS known/remembered Wi-Fi networks list.

tables.name: wifi_status
tables.description: macOS current WiFi status. This table requires Full Disk Access (FDA) permission to retrieve network_name.

tables.name: wifi_survey
tables.description: Scan for nearby WiFi networks.

tables.name: winbaseobj
tables.description: Lists named Windows objects in the default object directories, across all terminal services sessions. Example Windows object types include Mutexes, Events, Jobs and Semaphors.

tables.name: windows_crashes
tables.description: Extracted information from Windows crash logs (Minidumps).

tables.name: windows_eventlog
tables.description: Table for querying all recorded Windows event logs.

tables.name: windows_events
tables.description: Windows Event logs.

tables.name: windows_firewall_rules
tables.description: Provides the list of Windows firewall rules.

tables.name: windows_optional_features
tables.description: Lists names and installation states of windows features. Maps to Win32_OptionalFeature WMI class.

tables.name: windows_search
tables.description: Run searches against the Windows system index database using Advanced Query Syntax. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/search/-search-3x-advancedquerysyntax for details.

tables.name: windows_security_center
tables.description: The health status of Window Security features. Health values can be "Good", "Poor". "Snoozed", "Not Monitored", and "Error".

tables.name: windows_security_products
tables.description: Enumeration of registered Windows security products. Note: Not compatible with Windows Server.

tables.name: windows_update_history
tables.description: Provides the history of the windows update events.

tables.name: wmi_bios_info
tables.description: Lists important information from the system bios.

tables.name: wmi_cli_event_consumers
tables.description: WMI CommandLineEventConsumer, which can be used for persistence on Windows. See https://www.blackhat.com/docs/us-15/materials/us-15-Graeber-Abusing-Windows-Management-Instrumentation-WMI-To-Build-A-Persistent%20Asynchronous-And-Fileless-Backdoor-wp.pdf for more details.

tables.name: wmi_event_filters
tables.description: Lists WMI event filters.

tables.name: wmi_filter_consumer_binding
tables.description: Lists the relationship between event consumers and filters.

tables.name: wmi_script_event_consumers
tables.description: WMI ActiveScriptEventConsumer, which can be used for persistence on Windows. See https://www.blackhat.com/docs/us-15/materials/us-15-Graeber-Abusing-Windows-Management-Instrumentation-WMI-To-Build-A-Persistent%20Asynchronous-And-Fileless-Backdoor-wp.pdf for more details.

tables.name: xprotect_entries
tables.description: Database of the machine's XProtect signatures.

tables.name: xprotect_meta
tables.description: Database of the machine's XProtect browser-related signatures.

tables.name: xprotect_reports
tables.description: Database of XProtect matches (if user generated/sent an XProtect report).

tables.name: yara_events
tables.description: Track YARA matches for files specified in configuration data.

tables.name: yara_file NEW
tables.description: Triggers one-off YARA query for files at the specified path. Additionally requires one of `sig_group`, `sigfile`, or `sigrule`.

tables.name: yara_process NEW
tables.description: Triggers one-off YARA query for process memory of the specified pid. Additionally requires one of `sig_group`, `sigfile`, or `sigrule`.

tables.name: ycloud_instance_metadata
tables.description: Yandex.Cloud instance metadata.

tables.name: yum_sources
tables.description: Current list of Yum repositories or software channels.
